name: website-full-audit
description: Complete website audit by every department. Engineering, security, performance, UX, accessibility, SEO, content, and analytics each run in parallel — then a synthesis produces a 30/60/90-day roadmap with per-role improvement ownership.

config.instruction: You are auditing the codebase of a live website (not a PR). Review the architecture, tech debt, code quality, dependency hygiene, error handling, and maintainability. Treat the codebase description and any provided context as your diff. Deliver a prioritized list of engineering improvements.

Audit this website for security vulnerabilities: authentication weaknesses, exposed secrets, insecure dependencies, CORS misconfiguration, XSS/CSRF/injection risks, insecure headers, and data exposure. Rank findings by severity and provide remediation steps for each.

Audit the website for Core Web Vitals performance, asset optimization, caching, third-party script impact, and server-side bottlenecks. Deliver a ranked fix list with expected Lighthouse score impact.

Audit the website from a senior UX designer's perspective. Evaluate first impressions, information architecture, user flow friction, visual hierarchy, consistency, and micro-interactions. Deliver prioritized UX fixes.

Audit the website against WCAG 2.2 AA. Check perceivability, operability, understandability, and robustness. Identify every barrier for users with disabilities and deliver a remediation plan with code-level fixes.

Audit the website for SEO: site structure, meta tags, header hierarchy, internal linking, page speed signals, schema markup, content freshness, keyword targeting, and crawlability. Prioritize fixes by ranking impact.

Audit the website's messaging, value proposition clarity, CTA effectiveness, copy tone, trust signals, and content gaps. Rewrite the homepage headline and top CTAs. Deliver a content improvement roadmap.
Audit the website's conversion funnel, tracking setup, exit pages, and CRO opportunities. Map drop-off points, identify tracking gaps, and produce a ranked A/B test roadmap.

All 8 department audits have completed. Synthesize their findings into: (1) cross-audit pattern recognition, (2) impact × effort matrix, (3) a department-by-department roadmap with ownership assignments, (4) a 30/60/90-day implementation plan, (5) an executive summary, and (6) a scored audit scorecard for each department.
